key_insight: Large language models can speed up cancer registry abstraction, with the strongest models performing well on many structured variables but struggling more with exact date extraction.
clinical_relevance: This could reduce manual registrar workload and shorten the time needed to turn clinical records into usable population-level cancer surveillance data.
method: Evaluated 5 foundation LLMs in zero-shot prompting
Abstracted 8 registry variables from clinical reports
Tested 5,939 patients across 7 cancer types against hospital registry ground truth
evidence_signal: Across a large real-world dataset, the best models reached about 0.8 F1 on low- and high-cardinality registry variables, while exact date accuracy was about 0.55 and improved to about 0.85 with a ±30-day tolerance.
watch_item: Performance was uneven across variable types, so human review and careful validation are still needed before deployment in registry workflows.
paper_title: Large language models for cancer registry abstraction: a real-world evaluation across models, variables, and cancer types
